name: Community Legal Services Of Mid-Florida, Inc. - Kissimmee Office
description_short: Community Legal Services provides no-cost legal assistance to low-income individuals across Florida, offering support in family law, housing, financial matters, and consumer rights.
description_long:
Community Legal Services of Mid-Florida has operated for 60 years providing legal support to underserved populations across Florida. The organization operates multiple offices, including the Kissimmee location, and serves as a comprehensive legal aid provider with no-cost services to eligible clients. Their mission centers on enhancing lives through legal support, assistance, and education, addressing systemic barriers that prevent low-income individuals from accessing justice. The organization maintains strong community integration with regular clinic events and educational programming.

Community Legal Services offers free legal assistance across multiple practice areas including family law (divorce, custody, children's issues), housing matters (fair housing, landlord-tenant disputes), financial issues (bankruptcy, SNAP/food assistance navigation), small business and nonprofit formation, disaster relief, elder law, student and children's rights, and violence protection services. They provide both individual legal representation and group legal clinics, with virtual and in-person options. Specialized programming addresses frequent community needs like food assistance applications and disaster recovery support.

The organization distinguishes itself through its 60-year track record, multi-office infrastructure across Florida, accessible clinic model with scheduled events, virtual service delivery options, and deep specialization in issues affecting vulnerable populations (older adults, children, violence survivors). Their website clearly identifies service areas and provides calendar transparency for upcoming clinics. The focus on education and systemic issues rather than profit-driven solutions sets them apart in the legal services landscape.

A significant limitation is that services are restricted to income-eligible applicants, meaning not all consumers will qualify. The website does not provide clear intake procedures, eligibility thresholds, or response timelines. Geographic service areas may not cover all of Mid-Florida, and availability may be constrained by nonprofit funding. While the clinic schedule shows specific dates, there is no information about scheduling individual consultations or current wait times.
pros: Completely free legal services with no-cost representation for qualifying clients
60-year organizational track record providing legal aid across Florida
Multiple office locations including dedicated Kissimmee office
Wide range of practice areas including family law, housing, bankruptcy, and elder law
Virtual clinic options for remote accessibility
Transparent clinic calendar with specific dates and times published
Focus on underserved populations including children, elderly, and domestic violence survivors
cons: Limited to income-qualified applicants; eligibility requirements not clearly stated on website
No published intake procedure, wait times, or response time expectations
Geographic service area not clearly defined; may not serve all of Mid-Florida
Clinic-based model may limit availability compared to on-demand legal services
Website lacks information about case acceptance criteria or service limitations
best_for: Low-income individuals facing family law issues (divorce, custody, child support)
Renters and homeowners dealing with housing discrimination or landlord disputes
People seeking debt relief information or bankruptcy guidance
Domestic violence survivors needing legal protection and advocacy
diagnosis: Community Legal Services is best for income-qualified individuals in Florida seeking free legal representation in family, housing, bankruptcy, or consumer protection matters. The primary caveat is that eligibility is income-restricted and the organization operates as a nonprofit clinic model, so availability and response times may vary based on funding and demand.
services: Family law representation and clinics (divorce, custody, visitation, child support)
Housing advocacy (fair housing, landlord-tenant disputes, eviction defense)
Small business and nonprofit formation assistance
Domestic violence protection orders and victim advocacy
Virtual legal clinics for remote access
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
